entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Cultivated crop Arachis hypogaea L. characterized by a narrow genetic base and susceptibility to diseases and pests

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions that groundnut is susceptible to

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that cause damage to groundnut crops

entities.name: RESISTANCES
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand diseases and pests, which is lacking in the cultivated groundnut gene pool

entities.name: GENETIC BASE
entities.description: The level of genetic diversity within the cultivated gene pool of a crop

entities.name: A GENOME SPECIES
entities.description: Ancestral diploid species contributing the A genome to the evolution of groundnut

entities.name: B GENOME SPECIES
entities.description: Ancestral diploid species contributing the B genome to the evolution of groundnut

entities.name: TETRAPLOID GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Groundnut with four sets of chromosomes, including newly generated sources developed to broaden the genetic base

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Diploid species related to groundnut used to introduce useful traits and broaden the genetic base

entities.name: PLOIDY DIFFERENCES
entities.description: Differences in the number of chromosome sets between cultivated species and wild relatives

entities.name: AMPHIPLOIDS
entities.description: Polyploids created by combining different putative genomes

entities.name: AUTOTETRAPLOIDS
entities.description: Polyploids with four sets of chromosomes derived from the same species

entities.name: MOLECULAR DIVERSITY
entities.description: The level of genetic variation within the newly generated tetraploid groundnut population

entities.name: DART MARKERS
entities.description: Molecular markers used to assess the level of molecular diversity

relationships.description: exhibit differences compared to cultivated species
